global.summary: Ken Sargent House is a home away from home for patients receiving treatment at the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital and their families — a program of the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital Foundation.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Take in an aerial view of Ken Sargent House and the surrounding campus. From here you can see the full building layout, the main entrance, visitor parking, and the landscaped grounds that welcome patients and families. The house sits within walking distance of the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital, making it a convenient home away from home during treatment.
facts: The building has a main entrance on the front wooden deck.
Parking is available on the right side of the campus.
The facility is surrounded by green fields and landscaped grounds.
The house is within walking distance of the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital.

faqs.a: This is Ken Sargent House — a home away from home for patients and families accessing healthcare in Grande Prairie, supported by the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital Foundation.
The main entrance is on the front of the building, accessible via the wooden deck. Click the Main Entrance hotspot to go there.
Yes. Parking is located on the right side of the campus, visible from this overview.
Ken Sargent House is within walking distance of the Grande Prairie Regional Hospital — a short walk for patients and families.

kitchen.title: Kitchen
kitchen.description: The kitchen at Ken Sargent House is a shared space where guests and families can prepare meals, store food, and gather during their stay.
facts: The kitchen is a shared amenity for guests staying at Ken Sargent House.
Families can prepare meals and store food during extended stays.

faqs.a: Yes. The kitchen is a shared space for guests and families to prepare meals and store food during their stay at Ken Sargent House.

entrance-to-the-base-level.title: Entrance to the Base Level
entrance-to-the-base-level.description: Approach the base-level entrance of Ken Sargent House from the landscaped grounds. This lower entry provides access to guest spaces on the building's foundation level.
facts: The base-level entrance is on the lower side of the building.
Landscaped grounds surround the approach to this entry.

faqs.a: The base-level entrance is a lower entry to Ken Sargent House, accessible from the landscaped grounds. Use the Overview scene to see how it fits within the campus layout.

comfort-corner.title: Comfort Corner
comfort-corner.description: The Comfort Corner at Ken Sargent House offers a welcoming space where guests can pause, connect, and find a moment of calm during their stay.
facts: Comfort Corner is a welcoming lounge space for guests and families.

faqs.a: Comfort Corner is a welcoming space at Ken Sargent House where guests can pause, connect, and find a moment of calm during their stay.

main-entrance.title: Main Entrance
main-entrance.description: Arrive at the ground-level main entrance of Ken Sargent House. Glass doors open directly into the reception lobby, with an accessible ramp and railings on the left side of the front stairs. Illuminated welcome signage helps visitors find their way, whether arriving during the day or in the evening.
facts: ADA-accessible ramp is on the left side of the stairs.
Welcome signage is illuminated above the glass doors.
The entrance leads directly to the reception lobby.

faqs.a: Yes. A concrete ramp with white railings is located on the left side of the main stairs, suitable for wheelchair access.

faqs.q: How do I get to reception?
faqs.a: Enter through the glass doors ahead. Click the Reception hotspot or use the scene panel to navigate to the reception lobby.
Visitor parking is in the lot visible from the entrance area. Click the Parking Lot naming opportunity marker or return to Overview for a full campus view.

suggestedQuestions: How do I get to reception?

reception.title: Reception
reception.description: Step into the reception lobby at Ken Sargent House — the first point of contact for guests and families. Check in at the welcome desk, take a seat in the comfortable lounge area, and get directions to the dining hall and other guest spaces. Our reception team is here to help everyone feel informed, supported, and at home.
facts: Check in at the front desk with the WELCOME sign.
Dining Hall is to the left past the elevator.
A comfortable lounge area is available for visitors.

faqs.a: Check in at the reception desk on your right, marked with the WELCOME sign. Staff can assist with appointments and directions.
The Dining Hall is located to the left of the lobby, past the elevator area. Signage is visible from the reception area.

faqs.q: How do I go back outside?
faqs.a: Use the Main Entrance hotspot or the scene panel to return to the exterior entrance view.

suggestedQuestions: How do I go back outside?

overall.title: Overall
overall.description:
A Ken Sargent house is designed to blend comfort with modern elegance, and its covered porch is no exception. Extending seamlessly from the home's main living space, the covered porch serves as a perfect transition between indoors and outdoors.

Built with high-quality wood or composite materials, the porch is both stylish and durable, capable of withstanding various weather conditions. Large glass doors often lead to this space, allowing natural light to flood the interior while providing easy access to fresh air.

A signature feature of a Ken Sargent covered porch is its thoughtful design, incorporating subtle lighting and an inviting atmosphere. Whether used for relaxing mornings with a cup of coffee, sunset gatherings with friends, or peaceful moments under the stars, this porch enhances the overall experience of the home.

Designed with both aesthetics and functionality in mind, the covered porch is a place where architecture and nature come together, creating a serene and inviting retreat.
facts: Overall is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

dining-area.title: Dining Area
dining-area.description: The Dining Area at the Ken Sargent House is for more than sharing a meal, it’s a space for connection and togetherness. It’s where families gather around the table, share stories, and support each other. As the hub of the home, it provides more than just nourishment—it’s a place of comfort, where conversations are plenty and family and friends can visit, play games, and share in conversation. Your support helps us maintain this special space, ensuring that families have a place to eat, share, and build lasting memories during their time here.
facts: Dining Area is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

family-patient-communal-space.title: Family & Patient Communal Space
family-patient-communal-space.description:
The Family & Patient Communal Space at the Ken Sargent House is a haven designed to bring families together during difficult times to find comfort and connection, building a strong sense of community. This shared space fosters support and camaraderie, helping families navigate difficult times with emotional resilience. It’s a place, in the main area of the home where families share experiences, offer advice, and build lasting relationships that extend beyond their stay. 

This is where the magic of natural support systems are created, where strangers become friends and friends turn into family. Your support helps us maintain this vital space, ensuring families have a safe, supportive environment to heal together.
facts: Family & Patient Communal Space is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

accessible-larger-suites.title: Accessible & Larger Suites
accessible-larger-suites.description:
At the Ken Sargent House, our two accessible and two larger suites offer families a private sanctuary during their loved one’s medical treatment.

These larger suites provide privacy and comfort, giving families a personal space to rest, recharge, and find solace during challenging times.

The accessible & larger suites include all the basic necessities—two beds, a bathroom with a shower, and storage for personal belongings—making it a true home away from home.

Your support can help us maintain this vital space, offering families a safe, private and comforting environment during their time of need.
facts: Accessible & Larger Suites is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

suite-a.title: Suite A
suite-a.description:
At the Ken Sargent House, Suite A offers families a private sanctuary on the main level during their loved one’s medical treatment.

This 476 square foot suite provides privacy and comfort, giving families a personal space to rest, recharge, and find solace during challenging times.

With its convenient location near the hospital, Suite A  in the Ken Sargent House reduces travel stress and ensures families can stay close to their loved ones when it matters most.

        The suite includes all the basic necessities—two beds, a bathroom with a bathtub, and storage for personal belongings—making it a true home away from home.

Your support can help us maintain this vital space, offering families a safe and comforting environment during their time of need.

gazebo.title: Gazebo
gazebo.description:
The Gazebo at the Ken Sargent House offers a peaceful retreat, set against the serene backdrop of Northwestern Alberta’s breathtaking natural beauty. Surrounded by the quiet tranquility of the outdoors, it’s a space where families can unwind, reflect, and find peace amidst the challenges they face. 

For caregivers and families alike, this space provides the perfect opportunity to recharge, away from the clinical environment. Whether you’re sitting in the stillness of winter or enjoying the cool northern breeze, the Gazebo is a sanctuary that promotes healing and connection. 

Your support ensures that this tranquil retreat continues to serve families in need of respite.
facts: Gazebo is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

pantry.title: Pantry
pantry.description:
At the Ken Sargent House, our pantry is more than a place to store food—it’s a vital resource for families. The pantry offers convenient access to dry goods, snacks, and personal food storage, ensuring that families can focus on what matters most—being there for one another. 

It also alleviates financial burdens by offering nutritious, cost-effective options that support the well-being of our families. Whether it’s the long winters or the unpredictable weather, the pantry helps families maintain healthy eating habits during challenging times. 

Your support allows us to continue offering this essential service, providing comfort and relief when it’s needed most.
facts: Pantry is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
Use navigation markers to move between areas of Ken Sargent House.

playroom.title: Playroom
playroom.description:
The Playroom at the Ken Sargent House offers a burst of joy and creativity for children, allowing them to engage in playful activities and foster growth, even in the midst of adversity. 

In Northwestern Alberta, where winters are long and isolation can set in, the Playroom is a vital space where children can reduce stress, express themselves, and experience moments of happiness. This safe and welcoming space encourages social interactions, while giving children a sense of normalcy and fun. 

Your support helps keep this haven alive, offering children and families a much-needed escape and the chance to create memories of joy, even in difficult times.
facts: Playroom is part of the Ken Sargent House virtual tour.
